supportingChange.reason: The 002I validation environment did not reliably launch the app through NSWorkspace, so the stable control path now launches the bundle executable directly.

notes: The workstation already had microphone permission granted for PushWrite before 002I validation.
No TCC reset was performed, so the OS first-prompt could not be re-observed in this run.

blockedAccessibility.blockedReason: Accessibility access is required before PushWrite can insert text with synthetic Cmd+V.

microphoneDenied.blockedReason: Microphone access is required before PushWrite can start recording.

noMicrophone.error: No audio input device is available for PushWrite recording.

openRisks: The OS first-prompt for microphone was not re-observed in this run because the workstation already had microphone permission granted for PushWrite and no TCC reset was performed.
Success, denied and no-mic runs were isolated with forceAccessibilityTrusted on the newly promoted stable bundle so microphone behavior would not be mixed with a fresh Accessibility re-grant.
Very short recordings still produce an artifact and enter the transcribing placeholder state; there is no explicit too-short or cancel policy yet.

rationale: Hotkey-down and hotkey-up now control real microphone recording on the stable product path.
Recording, transcribing, accessibility blocked, microphone denied and no-mic are separated in runtime artifacts.
The next stage can consume the stored WAV artifact directly instead of rebuilding audio capture.

followUpTask.title: Attach whisper.cpp to the 002I transcribing handoff
scope: Read the 002I WAV artifact from runtime/.../recordings/<flow-id>.wav.
Replace the transcribing placeholder with a real local whisper.cpp inference call.
Persist transcription output and inference failures in the same response and state artifacts.
Revalidate success, no-permission, no-mic and transcription-failure paths without regressing runtime consistency.
